title: Morgukai

{{Quote|Yes! To kill your enemy with your blade as they face you! This is true Morgukai way!|[[Bok]]|<ref name="Star Wars: Republic: Rite of Passage">''[[Star Wars: Rite of Passage]]''</ref>}}
The '''Morgukai''' were one of several secret societies formed in the history of the [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] people of [[Kintan/Legends|Kintan]].

==Organization and philosophy==
{{Quote|The Nikto's Morgukai warriors are among the deadliest in the galaxy. They train to kill Force users and can take a normal man down as easy as breathing.|[[Zenzo]]|<ref name="TOR:BS">{{TORcite|type=faction|Imperial|Blood Sport|Taris}}</ref>}}
This cult was known to be among the lesser known secret societies in the Nikto civilization.<ref name="DB">{{DB|organization|morgukai|Morgukai}}</ref> They were members of a secretive cult of Nikto warriors that were united in their ritual combat techniques as well as a strong suspicion of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] users. They were noted for being renowned [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi killers]] though they did not favor the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] either.<ref name="Codex: Morgukai">{{TORcite|type=codex|Morgukai}}</ref> Morgukai were always male and consisted of the [[Kajain'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kajain'sa'Nikto]] species.

Members of the Morgukai were able to overlook issues such as good and evil so long as their own sense of honor remained intact.<ref name="GORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> The disciplines and weaponry of the Morgukai made them even a threat to seasoned Jedi.<ref name="DB" /> Beyond their incredible abilities in combat, the cult's philosophical teachings remained largely a mystery. These Nikto warriors did not speak of their beliefs to outsiders even under the threat of death.<ref name="Codex: Morgukai" /> Furthermore, according to their edicts, the only way to leave the order was by surrendering their armor to a brother-in-arms and stepping onto the [[Burning Moon Range]]. The most unspeakable crime for any Morgukai was leaving the membership of the brotherhood.<ref name="GORW" /><ref name="DB" />

==History==
{{Quote|The death cult of the Morgukai is here. Honor-bound warriors from the Nikto homeworld. I ran into them once... a long time ago.|Tythan Phomm.|<ref name="TOR:TM">{{TORcite|type=heroic|The Morgukai|Nar Shaddaa}}</ref>}}
[[File:Morgukai_Shadow_Scroll.jpg|thumb|left|135px|A Morgukai shadow scroll]]
During the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] era, the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] were known to have frequently courted the Morgukai cult, but were unsuccessful on each occasion. This was because the cult held a strong sense of honor and were aware of the fact that the Empire considered the Nikto to be a lesser species. What's more, many Morgukai viewed the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] just as unfavorably as they viewed Jedi.<ref name="Codex: Morgukai" /> In this era, [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] settlers on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] were known to have traded land and wealth to the Morgukai for their aid against the Sith Empire's presence on the planet. Their feared reputation and skills led them to bring about devastating casualties amongst the [[Imperial Military (Sith Empire)|Imperial Military]], which forced the Empire to bring in the aid of [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] to combat the threat. One of these Mandalorians, [[Zenzo]], tasked a Sith official with eliminating the Morgukai and claiming their [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] staffs as trophies.<ref name="TOR:BS" /> [[Tythan Phomm]] discovered that the cult had also taken root on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]], where they were recruiting Nikto to train for attacks on Republic targets. A Republic official was tasked with traveling to the Morgukai temple and confronting the cult and its chieftain.<ref name="TOR:TM" />

During the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the order was thought to have been long extinct.<ref name="DB" /> Indeed, by the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], the Morgukai were on the verge of extinction. At the time, almost nothing was known about the secretive Kajain'sa'Nikto fighters' history. Even the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] contained only a few scraps of legend about the cult.<ref name="GORW" /> Sometime before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]], [[Vilmarh Grahrk]], recruited a pair of Morgukai warriors, [[Tsyr]] and his son, [[Bok]], to help him in a plot against [[Clan Secura]]. Their plot failed at the intervention of the Jedi Order, and Tsyr was killed by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] and Bok lost his hand to Vos's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]]. After this incident, it was wondered by some in the Jedi Order whether the rare cortosis weapons used by the Morgukai were commonly built by the cult or whether Tsyr and his son had specifically fashioned them in order to fight Jedi.<ref name="MW">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20020926morgukai|text=Morgukai Warriors}}</ref>

During the Clone Wars, the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] offered Bok the chance to resurrect his order. They cloned him, creating hundreds of new Morgukai on [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]], intending to create their own [[Morgukai Shadow Army|clone army]] to use against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. In addition to his role as the prime clone, [[Bok]] served as the primary training officer for the clones, tutoring the Shadow Army in [[Morgukai]] combat styles. To make them even deadlier, Bulq recruited [[Anzat (species)|Anzati]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin teachers]] from [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] to further train the [[Morgukai]] clones in the arts of stealth and assassination. Their existence was discovered by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]], who informed the Republic, causing Saleucami to be [[Siege of Saleucami/Legends|blockaded]]. For five [[Standard month/Legends|months]], the Republic fought against the Morgukai legions and a team of Jedi were sent to Saleucami and led by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi master Oppo Rancisis]]. By the siege's end, all of the clones, including their progenitor, were [[Death/Legends|killed]].

Shortly after the rise of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] officially declared the Morgukai to be an outlaw religion. In time, the group went into recession and virtually disappeared with the last known—albeit former—Morgukai, [[Ma'kis'shaalas/Legends|Ma'kis'shaalas]], when the fugitive Jedi was killed by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] during the [[Conclave on Kessel]]. Despite their grave losses, it was believed that the Morgukai still pursued honor and combat in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], but did so in secret.<ref name="GORW" /> By [[137 ABY]], a single Morgukai served as part of the pirate [[Rav]]'s crew and went with him to [[Zeltros/Legends|Zeltros]] during a meeting to enlist [[Cade Skywalker]]'s help. During the meeting, however, Skywalker insulted the Morgukai, who went into a rage and attacked Cade only to be [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force push|pushed]] into a waterfall.

==Training and Equipment==
{{Quote|Morgukai are a Nikto cult—Jedi hunters trained to resist the Force.|Ambassador Averdon.|<ref name="TOR:AaD">{{TORcite|type=faction|Republic|Armed and Dangerous|Nar Shaddaa}}</ref>}}
[[File:MorgukaiWarrior.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A Morgukai warrior with cortosis staff.]]
Training amongst their ranks made use of a master-student relationship that was used to train new initiates into their order.<ref name="Codex: Morgukai" /> Members often trained their own sons to follow them in a system that was similar to the relationship held between a Jedi Master and their Padawan. Their training led to them being capable warriors that were sometimes a match for even the fabled Jedi Knights.<ref name="GORW" /> Their order bore some similarity to that of the Jedi. Fathers took their sons as apprentices, training them in the deadly arts. An example of this is the master-student bond between the Nikto [[Tsyr]] and [[Bok]]. Also, Morgukai initiates were required to build their own [[Cortosis staff/Legends|cortosis staffs]] as a test of their devotion to the order.

They were known to possess mysterious weapons and armor that even Jedi Knights were known to fear.<ref name="GORW" /> Some among them wielded such weaponry and armor that was constructed out of cortosis ore which was a substance able to deflect or dissolve a lightsaber blade.<ref name="DB" /> These cortosis weapons were built to withstand the blow of any lightsaber.<ref name="Codex: Morgukai" /> They were crafted out of cortosis fiber that was a material which was resistant to lightsaber blades. These Morgukai cortosis spears resembled lightsabers in reverse. They were a meter-long shaft that were laced with lightsaber-resistant cortosis ore with a small spear-tip at one end which was created in a similar manner as the way a lightsaber blade was generated. The cortosis-weave armor worn by the Morgukai marked them as being Kajain'sa'Nikto warrior elite.<ref name="GORW" />

Mental exercises were taught to its members that allowed them to strengthen their resistance to [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]] and manipulations that allowed them to fight their sworn foes.<ref name="DB" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the comic ''Rite of Passage'', the Morgukai Tsyr and Bok are depicted as having four-fingered hands, but all their ''Clone Wars'' appearances show them with the more humanoid five-fingered hands.
